evals.name: thorough-pr-review
evals.prompt: Can you do a thorough review of PR #142? It's a fairly big change — adds a background job queue with a worker pool and retries. I want to know if it's ready to merge.
evals.expected_output: Resolves the PR target (gh pr view/diff #142), recognizes the scope is substantial so picks the `full` preset, runs the lenses (adaptively — likely parallel subagents given size), and produces a single synthesized review with the prescribed structure: executive summary with an explicit merge recommendation, P0/P1/P2 issues with file:line refs, wiring/commit/test-quality sections, positives, recommendations. Deduplicates cross-lens findings.

evals.name: security-focused-small-diff
evals.prompt: I just changed how we validate and store API tokens in auth/tokens.py and auth/store.py — about 80 lines. Before I merge, give it a security review.
evals.expected_output: Picks the `security-focused` preset (security/resilience/correctness/wiring) because it's auth code. Small scope, so runs the lenses inline/sequentially rather than spawning subagents. Synthesized review focuses on injection, secret handling, validation gaps, with explicit merge recommendation and file:line references.

evals.name: refactor-review-branch
evals.prompt: I refactored the reporting module on branch refactor/reporting-cleanup — behavior should be unchanged, I just split the god-class up. Review it.
evals.expected_output: Diffs the branch against main, recognizes this is a behavior-preserving refactor and picks the `refactor` preset (elegance/smells/style/commit-discipline). Synthesized review judges whether the split actually improved the design, flags remaining smells, and comments on commit atomicity. Doesn't over-index on new-feature concerns like performance/security since behavior is unchanged.

evals.name: review-a-tracked-change
evals.prompt: Review the changes on branch feature/upload-handler (diff against main) for merge-readiness. This work is tracked as beads issue proj-7f.
evals.expected_output: Runs the multi-lens review and synthesis as usual, then records the outcome on proj-7f: the verdict (merge / not) and the blocking items go into proj-7f's notes so the result lives with the work. Does not build epics/dependency graphs or lecture about bd usage.
assertions: A correct preset is chosen and a synthesized prioritized review is produced
The verdict and blocking items are recorded in proj-7f's notes
Does not invent a bd epic/dependency graph or re-teach bd usage
